subject: regex `{` character
new: `{` must be escaped as `\{` in regex
truth: Stop using unescaped `{` in regex patterns; escape it as `\{` or use `--relaxed-re-syntax`
quote: The `{` character must be explicitly escaped when used outside repetition operators. YARA 4.x: `/abc{/` treats `{` as literal. YARA-X: `/abc{/` is invalid; must write `/abc\{/`

from_fact: `{` treated as literal in regex
`{` must be escaped as `\{` in regex

subject: invalid regex escape sequences
old: invalid escape sequences silently fall back to literal character
truth: Stop relying on invalid regex escape sequences like `\g` silently becoming the literal character; they now cause errors unless `--relaxed-re-syntax` is used
quote: Invalid escape sequences now trigger errors instead of silent fallback. YARA 4.x behavior example: In `/foo\gbar/`, the invalid `\g` sequence becomes `g`, producing `/foogbar/`

from_fact: invalid escape sequences silently fall back to literal character
invalid escape sequences trigger errors

subject: base64 pattern minimum length
old: no minimum length for base64 patterns
truth: Stop using base64 patterns shorter than 3 characters; they are now rejected
quote: YARA-X requires patterns ≥ 3 characters (YARA 4.x has no minimum)

from_fact: no minimum length for base64 patterns
base64 patterns must be ≥ 3 characters

subject: base64/base64wide alphabet modifier
old: same alphabet required for both `base64` and `base64wide` in one string
new: different alphabets allowed for `base64` and `base64wide` in same string
truth: Stop assuming `base64` and `base64wide` must share the same alphabet when both are used on one string; YARA-X allows different alphabets
quote: Different alphabets allowed for `base64` and `base64wide` in same string. Invalid in YARA 4.x: `$a = "foo" base64 base64wide("./ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789")`. Valid in YARA-X: `base64` uses default alphabet; `base64wide` uses custom alphabet

from_fact: same alphabet required for both `base64` and `base64wide` in one string
different alphabets allowed for `base64` and `base64wide` in same string

subject: wildcards in `of` statement rule names
new: wildcards in `of` statement rule names are invalid
truth: Stop using wildcards (e.g. `some_rule*`) in `of` statements; they are no longer supported
quote: Wildcards with rule names removed. Valid: `1 of (some_rule)`. Invalid: `1 of (some_rule*)`

from_fact: wildcards like `some_rule*` allowed in `of` statements
wildcards in `of` statement rule names are invalid
e.g

subject: XOR + fullword combination
old: bytes before/after XORed pattern are NOT XORed before alphanumeric check
new: bytes before/after XORed pattern ARE XORed before alphanumeric check
truth: Stop expecting `xor(1) fullword` to match patterns where surrounding bytes are not XORed; YARA-X XORs the context bytes before checking for full-word boundaries
quote: Bytes before/after XORed pattern are also XORed before alphanumeric check. Example: `"mississippi" xor(1) fullword` matching `{lhrrhrrhqqh}` is invalid in YARA-X because XORing yields `zmississippiz` (searches for full words in XORed context)

from_fact: bytes before/after xored pattern are not xored before alphanumeric check
bytes before/after xored pattern are xored before alphanumeric check

subject: negative array indexes
truth: Stop using negative array indexes like `@a[-1]`; they now cause errors instead of returning undefined
quote: `@a[-1]` is now an error (was `undefined` in YARA 4.x)

from_fact: `@a[-1]` returns undefined
`@a[-1]` is an error

subject: hex pattern jump bounds
old: jump bounds only accept base-10 values
new: jump bounds accept hex and octal values
truth: Stop assuming hex pattern jump bounds like `[0x00-0x100]` are invalid; YARA-X now accepts hex and octal values in jump bounds
quote: Bounds accept hex and octal values (base-10 only in YARA 4.x). Now valid: `{ 01 02 03 [0x00-0x100] 04 05 06 }`

from_fact: jump bounds only accept base-10 values
jump bounds accept hex and octal values

subject: duplicate rule modifiers
new: each `global` or `private` modifier can appear only once
truth: Stop using duplicate `global` or `private` modifiers on rules; YARA-X rejects `global global global rule ...`
quote: Each `global` or `private` modifier can appear only once. Invalid in YARA-X: `global global global rule duplicated_global { ... }`

from_fact: duplicate `global`/`private` modifiers allowed
each `global` or `private` modifier can appear only once

subject: import name
truth: Replace `import yara` with `import yara_x` when migrating from yara-python to yara-x.
quote: Note the import name is `yara_x` (underscore), replacing the old `import yara` from yara-python.

from_fact: import yara
import yara_x
